name: Mechanism predicting the PMNS lepton mixing pattern from structural inputs
closability_note: Modular and family symmetries (A_4, S_4, A_5, modular Gamma_N for N <= 5) explicitly predict near-tri-bimaximal or near-trimaximal mixing patterns; many models in the literature give theta_12 ~ 35 deg, theta_23 ~ 45 deg, theta_13 < 5 deg as group-theoretic outputs. The 2012 measurement of nonzero theta_13 ~ 8.5 deg killed pure tri-bimaximal, but the modular-symmetry program has adjusted: certain modular weights at level 3 give the corrected angles. The gap is plausibly closable: at least 3-4 published modular-flavor models reproduce all 6 PMNS observables (3 angles, 1 Dirac CP phase, plus mass-squared splittings) within current uncertainties. The remaining work is to show uniqueness — i.e. that the model is not just one of many parameter choices.
summary: The PMNS mixing matrix has a specific structure (near-trimaximal in atmospheric sector, large solar angle, nonzero but small reactor angle, CP phase ~ -90 deg) that no framework in the catalog derives. Modular flavor symmetries are the most actively-explored route.
what_closes_it: A mechanism that predicts all 3 PMNS angles and the Dirac CP phase from group-theoretic inputs — a finite modular group at level N, a set of representations, and modular weights — with no remaining free parameters beyond an overall mass scale. Candidate routes: (a) modular Gamma_3 ~ A_4 with specific weight assignments (Feruglio-Romanino 2018; Kobayashi-Tanaka-Tatsuishi 2018); (b) modular Gamma_4 ~ S_4 (Penedo-Petcov 2018); (c) modular Gamma_5 ~ A_5 (Novichkov et al. 2019). The closing mechanism would also have to predict (or at least constrain) the absolute neutrino-mass scale and the Majorana phases, which most current modular models leave as inputs. Compatibility with thermal leptogenesis adds further constraints.
scale_hint: Seesaw scale (10^9 - 10^14 GeV) for the right-handed-neutrino sector; modular vacuum value tau set at the GUT or compactification scale.

principles_at_stake.note: PMNS mixing breaks lepton-flavor conservation in the active-neutrino sector. The closing mechanism must reproduce the observed mixing while respecting the charged-lepton-sector LFU constraints (mu -> e gamma, etc.).

literature_anchors.citation: Kobayashi, Tanaka, Tatsuishi, 'Neutrino mixing from finite modular groups', PRD 98, 016004 (2018)
Penedo and Petcov, 'Lepton masses and mixing from modular S_4 symmetry', Nucl. Phys. B 939, 292 (2019)
Ding and King, 'Neutrino mass and mixing with modular symmetry', Rept. Prog. Phys. 87, 084201 (2024)
